title: 11 Herbal Creams For Upset Stomach
intro_desc: Herbal creams and teas can provide relief for an upset stomach. But let's focus on teas for now. These teas work because they contain properties that help soothe and calm the stomach muscles.  One example is Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger. It contains compounds that help reduce inflammation in the stomach and improve digestion. This can bring quick relief from nausea and stomach cramps.  Another example is Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. It's rich in antioxidants that help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate heartburn and stomach pain.  Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is another example. Its menthol content helps relax the stomach muscles and improve digestion. This can bring relief from bloating and gas.  Drinking these teas can bring several benefits to your life. They can help you avoid over-the-counter medications or prescription drugs that might have side effects. They can also help you maintain a healthy balance of gut bacteria, which is essential for a strong immune system.  Moreover, these teas are often easy to incorporate into your daily routine. You can make them at home or purchase them at a local store. They're also versatile, and you can add them to your meals or drink them on their own.  However, it's worth noting that if you have a severe or persistent stomach issue, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. They can recommend the best course of action for your specific situation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains bioactive constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and relax the muscles in the stomach. Ginger's warming properties increase blood flow to the stomach, which helps to reduce nausea and vomiting. The volatile oils in ginger, such as zingiberene and bisabolol, have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Zingiber officinale creams can provide relief for an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of ginger essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave for 30-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until melted.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined. Remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken. Once thickened, add ginger essential oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool completely before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is responsible for its soothing properties. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and discomfort in the stomach. The cream also contains flavonoids and saponins, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage. The saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra cream help to increase the mucus production in the stomach, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and a small saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan over low heat until warm. Remove from heat.
Add the glycerin and glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the warm water. Stir well until the powder dissolves.
Let the mixture cool down to room temperature. Strain it into a clean container using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add a preservative and stir well. Pour the mixture into small containers and store in the refrigerator. Use as needed for upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains bioactive constituents like menthol, menthone, and limonene. These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort. Menthol, in particular, helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract and increase blood flow, which can ease cramps and spasms. Menthone has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting. When applied topically as a cream, Mentha x piperita can provide fast relief from upset stomach symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of peppermint essential oil, and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove melted mixture from heat and stir in peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour mixture into small jars or containers and let cool until solid.
Label and store creams in a cool, dry place to use as needed for upset stomach relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains active constituents like fenchone, anethole, and limonene. These compounds have antispasmodic and carminative properties, which help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce gas and cramps. Fenchone and anethole also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an irritated stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Limonene has a relaxing effect on the digestive system and can help improve digestion. The combination of these properties in Foeniculum vulgare creams may help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach, such as bloating, cramps, and nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare (fennel seeds) and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Steep the fennel seeds in the carrier oil for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Discard the fennel seeds.
Add 10 drops of peppermint essential oil to the fennel-infused oil for better taste.
Transfer the cream to a glass jar and store it in the fridge for up to 6 months. Apply 1-2 teaspoons as needed to soothe an upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains compounds like quinine and cinchonine, alkaloids that have been used to treat upset stomach for centuries. Quinine, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the stomach. Cinchonine, on the other hand, has antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate cramps. The cream also contains tannins, which have a soothing effect on the stomach lining and help reduce inflammation. By applying these active constituents topically, Cinchona officinalis creams can provide quick relief from stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 1 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Grind the dried Cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Melt the coconut oil in the double boiler. Add the powdered Cinchona officinalis bark and stir well.
Heat the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then remove from heat and let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Transfer the mixture to a jar and store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a group of flavonoids and lignans, including silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties and protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. Silymarin also has antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent cell damage in the stomach. Additionally, silibinin has been shown to have a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause an upset stomach. By protecting the stomach lining and reducing inflammation, Silybum marianum creams can provide relief for people experiencing stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Silybum marianum flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the beeswax to the mixture and stir until it's fully melted and combined, then pour the mixture into a small tin or jar to cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid and valepotriates, which are bioactive constituents that help calm the stomach. These compounds have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms. Valerenic acid has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which helps to slow down digestion and reduce nausea. The valepotriates in Valeriana officinalis creams also have a protective effect on the stomach lining, preventing irritation and ulcers. By reducing inflammation and calming the stomach, Valeriana officinalis creams can provide relief from upset stomach and other digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root to the melted oil and wax mixture.
Remove from heat and let it steep for 2 hours to allow the root to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids. Let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains aloin, aloe-emodin, and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach. Aloe-emodin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause stomach upset. The gel-like texture of aloe vera also helps to protect and coat the stomach lining, preventing irritation and allowing it to heal naturally. The combination of these properties makes aloe barbadensis creams a popular natural remedy for soothing an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel from a mature plant.
Mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a saucepan.
Melt the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax dissolves.
Combine the Aloe gel, melted coconut oil mixture, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil in a bowl.
Pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate until it sets, then use as a cream for upset stomach relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains the bioactive constituents curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system. The curcuminoids in Curcuma longa creams have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which can cause stomach pain and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants in Curcuma longa creams help protect the stomach lining from damage and promote the healing of any ulcers or irritation. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Curcuma longa creams can help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa), 2 tablespoons of lavender oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Add the turmeric powder and stir well. Then, add the lavender oil and vitamin E oil and mix until combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 130°F. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains several bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Aloe-emodin has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help combat bacterial overgrowth and yeast infections in the stomach. Acemannan, a polysaccharide, stimulates the immune system and promotes the healing of stomach ulcers. The gel of the aloe vera plant also contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that can help calm the stomach and promote digestion.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of honey, 1 teaspoon of lemon juice, and a small bowl or container.
Mix aloe vera gel and coconut oil in the bowl until well combined.
Add honey and lemon juice to the bowl and mix well until smooth.
Pour the mixture into the small bowl or container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to chill.
Apply the aloe vera cream to the stomach area as needed to soothe and calm the upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica creams contains angelic acid and ferulic acid, two active constituents that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the stomach, making it easier to digest food. The cream also contains polysaccharides, which can soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. Angelica archangelica creams have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including upset stomachs and nausea, due to its anti-spasmodic properties that can relax the stomach muscles. By reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining, Angelica archangelica creams may provide relief from stomach upset and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal creams for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Angelica archangelica roots and 2 cups of water. Clean and chop the roots.
Boil the water in a pot. Add the chopped roots and let it boil for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the roots. Let it cool down.
Mix 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of honey in a bowl.
Add the cooled down Angelica archangelica mixture to the bowl and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool down and harden before using it as a cream for upset stomach.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease an upset stomach. Peppermint tea, in particular, has been known to calm the digestive system. Ginger tea is another option that can soothe stomach discomfort. These teas work by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
faq_safe: You can safely drink herbal teas for an upset stomach every day, but in moderation. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can help calm your stomach. However, overdoing it with strong herbs like chamomile or licorice root can lead to stomach problems. Listen to your body and adjust your tea consumption as needed.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to help with an upset stomach within 30 minutes to an hour after drinking. Some people may notice relief from discomfort and bloating within this timeframe. The effects can last for a few hours, but it's best to drink the tea regularly to maintain relief and soothe the stomach.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for an upset stomach can be soothing. For best results, try having a cup in the morning or early afternoon when your stomach is likely to be most sensitive. Avoid drinking too close to bedtime, as it can disrupt your sleep. A gentle cup of herbal tea can help calm your stomach and ease discomfort.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for an upset stomach. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, can make certain medications less effective. Others, like licorice root, can increase the risk of side effects. This is because herbs can change the way medications are processed in the body, affecting how they work.
faq_pregnant: When you have an upset stomach while pregnant, herbal teas might sound like a soothing solution. But, it's best to be cautious. Some herbs like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile are often considered safe in moderation. However, others like pennyroyal, blue cohosh, and red clover should be avoided as they might not be good for you or your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a gentle way to ease an upset stomach while breastfeeding. However, some herbs can pass into breastmilk and affect the baby. Peppermint, chamomile, and ginger teas are often considered safe, but it's best to check the tea's ingredients and choose a brand that's free from additives and caffeine.
title_2: Tourist Troubles: 11 Herbal Creams to Ease Upset Stomach
